informal: Show that a connected metric space having more than one point is uncountable.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_27_4
  {X : Type*} [MetricSpace X] [ConnectedSpace X] (hX : ∃ x y : X, x ≠ y) :
  ¬ Countable (univ : Set X)
reasoning: The Lean signature uses `MetricSpace X` and `ConnectedSpace X`, matching "connected metric space". The hypothesis `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` correctly captures "more than one point". The conclusion `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` says the underlying set is uncountable, which is equivalent to saying the type X is uncountable. This matches the informal statement exactly with no extra or missing hypotheses.
